source.definition: The fifth day of the week, following Thursday and before Saturday.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "Friday" originates from Old English 'Frīgedæg', named after the Norse goddess Frigg, associated with love and beauty.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „Friday” provine din engleza veche 'Frīgedæg', numit după zeița nordică Frigg, asociată cu dragostea și frumusețea.

target.definition: Ziua a cincea a săptămânii, după joi și înainte de sâmbătă.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "vineri" derives from the Latin 'Veneris dies', meaning 'day of Venus', named after the Roman goddess of love.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „vineri” provine din latinescul 'Veneris dies', care înseamnă 'ziua zeiței Venus', zeița romană a iubirii.

examples.sourceText: I have the day off on Friday.
Friday is the last working day of the week for many people.
examples.targetText: Vineri este ultima zi de muncă a săptămânii pentru mulți oameni.

examples.sourceText: We are meeting on Friday evening.
Friday is a good day for a trip.
examples.targetText: Vineri este o zi bună pentru o excursie.

learningTips.text: Remember "vineri" starts with 'v' like Venus, the Roman goddess of love, which the day is named after.
The English word "Friday" is linked to the Norse goddess Frigg, similar to how "vineri" relates to Venus, both goddesses of love.
Think of "vineri" as the day dedicated to love, just like Friday in English.
Imagine celebrating love on Friday (Frigg's day) to help remember the Romanian word "vineri".
